name: Layout & Grid
description: How the product listing grid and overall page layout should support efficient product browsing and comparison

heuristics.principle: The product grid should present items in a consistent, scannable layout that supports rapid visual comparison
checkpoints: Product cards are uniformly sized and aligned in a clear grid
Grid spacing is consistent between rows and columns
Card layout does not shift or break across viewport sizes
Visual weight is balanced so no single card dominates unless intentionally promoted

heuristics.principle: The page should provide a clear visual hierarchy distinguishing category context, filters and product results
checkpoints: Category heading or breadcrumb is visible above the product grid
Filters and sorting controls are visually separated from product results
Product count or result summary is displayed near the top
Promotional banners do not push the first product row below the fold

heuristics.principle: Users should be able to switch between grid and list view to match their browsing preference
checkpoints: View toggle control is visible near the product grid
List view provides additional product details compared to grid
View preference persists across pagination or navigation
Both views maintain consistent information hierarchy

heuristics.principle: The number of products per row should be appropriate for the viewport width without causing horizontal scrolling
checkpoints: Desktop shows 3-5 products per row depending on card content
Tablet adapts to 2-3 products per row
Mobile shows 1-2 products per row with adequate detail
No horizontal scrolling is required to view the full grid

heuristics.principle: White space and visual breathing room should prevent the listing from feeling cluttered or overwhelming
checkpoints: Adequate padding exists between product cards
Text within cards does not feel cramped or truncated
The page does not feel visually overwhelming with too many elements
Key information on each card is readable without effort
